fields.name: UpdateWarning
fields.fieldText:
You are about update the duration for this work type.

{!foundWT.Name}

Current Duration: {!foundWT.DurationInMinutes}

Recommend Duration: 85

New Duration: {!Enter_new_value_for_Duration}

fields.name: dt1
fields.fieldText:
Work Type: {!DRCWorkType}


32 completed work orders were found for this work type.


On average, the difference between Actual Duration and Estimated Duration is -35.66 minutes. A negative number indicates that work is being completed faster than planned.


All times below are in Minutes


Average Actual Duration:  84.34

Average Estimated Duration: 120

Total Actual Duration: 2,699

Total Estimated Duration: 3,840

Minimum Delta: -45

Maximum Delta: 15

Current Work Type Duration {!foundWT.DurationInMinutes} minutes


Suggested new Duration: 85 minutes

validationRule.errorMessage: Please enter a positive number for the new duration

formulas.name: SummaryMessage
formulas.expression: If(isblank({!Hours_Offset}), "The new demo date is " & text( {!New_Demo_Date}), "The appointments have been moved by " & text( {!Hours_Offset}) & " hours")

fields.name: OtherNextSeps
fields.fieldText:
Important Next Steps
Moving locations: If desired, change the addresses of the service territories and the timezones to match your requirements. For example, move territories from a California based address to a London one and change the timezones from Pacific time to Greenwich Mean Time (Europe/London). This can be found on the service territory record.

fields.name: drwnt
fields.fieldText: Demo records were not found in your org.  Please create at least one Service Appointment where the LS Demo Master flag is set to True.

fields.name: New_Demo_Date
fields.fieldText: New Demo Date (Must be a Monday)

validationRule.errorMessage: Please enter a new demo date or an hours offset, but not both.

fields.name: TimeZoneShiftingInfo
fields.fieldText: Alternatively, enter the number of hours, positive or negative, that you would like to shift the appointments by.  Use this option if you are located in another time zone other than Pacific Time and want to demo in that time zone.  Run this option if your appointments appear outside the normal work hours of your employee.

fields.name: Hours_Offset
fields.helpText: Enter the number of hours, positive or negative, that you would like to shift the appointments by.  Use this option if you are located in another time zone other than Pacific Time and want to demo in that time zone.

recordCreates.description: Creates a time sheet entry record, setting the category to DriveTime, the cost center ID to the ParentCostCenterId, the end time to the current date and time, the job expense type ID to the ParentJobExpenseTypeId, the start time to TimeSheetStartTime, the status to New, the subject to the DefaultSubjectForTimeSheetEntryDriveTime, the time sheet ID to the TimeSheetId, the work order ID to ParentWorkOrderId, the work order line item ID to ParentWorkOrderLineItemId, and the Time Block Sequence to TimeBlockSequenceDriveTimeFormula.
recordCreates.name: CreateTimeSheetEntryDriveTime
recordCreates.label: Create Time Sheet Entry

recordCreates.description: Creates a time sheet entry record with the category set to WorkTime, the cost center ID set to the ParentCostCenterId, the Job Expense Type ID set to the ParentJobExpenseTypeId, the start time set to TimeSheetStartTime, the status set to New, the subject set to the DefaultSubjectForTimeSheetEntryDriveTime, and the time sheet ID matching the TimeSheetId passed into the flow, the work order ID set to ParentWorkOrderId, the Work Order Line Item ID set to ParentWorkOrderLineItemId, and and the Time Block Sequence to TimeBlockSequenceDriveTimeFormula.
recordCreates.name: CreateTimeSheetEntryWorkTime
recordCreates.label: Create Time Sheet Entry

assignments.description: The loop node is here for cosmetic purposes. It helps make the flow easier to read, but is not a necessary technical component.
assignments.name: Loop
assignments.label: Loop

decisions.description: The Debug Screen Decision node determines whether to show the debug screen. The default is no.
decisions.name: Debug_Screen_Decision
decisions.label: Show Debug Screen?

decisions.description:
The Main Decision Hub is the primary driver of the checkout flow.

It uses the following logic:
 - If mainCheckoutSession.IsProcessing = True, go to the wait screen.
 - Otherwise, for each session.state, go to the corresponding subflow.
 
Note: The nextState is passed into the subflow and the nextState value is set to mainCheckoutSession.state when: 
 - An asynchronous invocable action completes in an asynchronous subflow.
 - Or the Save Session node produces the nextState value for a synchronous subflow, and assigns it to the nextState variable in the main flow. 

Asynchronous invocable actions set mainCheckoutSession.isProcess = True.
decisions.name: Main_Decision_Hub
decisions.label: Main Decision Hub

decisions.description: The Wait Debug Decision node determines whether to show the debug screen. The default is no.
decisions.name: Wait_Debug_Decision
decisions.label: Show Wait Debug?

screens.description: The Wait Debug Screen node displays a version of the wait screen with debug information.
screens.name: Wait_Debug_Screen
screens.label: Wait Debug

fields.name: WaitingLabel
fields.fieldText: Waiting Screen ( replace with a subflow at some point )

screens.description:
The Wait Screen node displays when session.isProcessing = true

Async subflows set session.isProcessing = true before calling async invocable actions.

When async invocable actions complete, they change session.isProcessing to false and set session.state to session.nextState, not the flow variable nextState.
screens.name: Wait_Screen
screens.label: Wait
